steps.description: Confirm the target commit to revert (read-only). Verify the commit hash, its parent, the full commit message, and the exact list of files the commit added or modified so we know what will be affected by the revert. Read those files to inspect what changed before performing any revert.

Create a safety backup branch from the current HEAD so we can restore the current state if anything goes wrong. This is non-destructive and fast. Choose a descriptive branch name (e.g. backup/revert-10f0a89). No source files are changed by creating the branch, but we reference the files that will be affected by the revert so we know what we backed up.

Attempt an automatic revert of the target commit using git revert. Run git revert <commit-hash> (optionally --no-edit) to create a new commit that undoes the changes from commit 10f0a89f3baa2bb9f2c0e6beae3cced1bd0489b5. If git revert succeeds cleanly, the revert commit will be created and no further file-level restores are required. The files listed are those expected to be changed by the revert.

If git revert produces conflicts (or if you prefer an explicit file-level restoration), restore every file that was added/modified by the commit to the version from the parent commit (ce07cd3e859a9aab09a11b9cdd598557548faecd). Use git restore --source=<parent> -- <path> or git checkout <parent> -- <path> for each file. This explicitly reverts the exact files changed by the commit.

After revert or manual restores, inspect the working tree and commit history to confirm the revert succeeded. Run git status, git --no-pager log -n 10 --pretty=format:'%H %an %ai %s', and git show <revert-commit> (if a revert commit was made) to review the diffs. Verify the listed files no longer contain the changes introduced in 10f0a89f3b....

If git revert was run with --no-commit or if manual restores were used and changes are currently staged/unstaged in the working tree, commit the revert (or the manual restores) with a clear message that references the original commit being reverted (e.g. "revert: undo 10f0a89f3b - feat: shipyard run ..."). Ensure author/committer details are appropriate.

If anything went wrong or you want to fully restore the repository to the pre-revert state, restore from the safety backup branch created in step 1. This will restore all refs and files to the exact state before the revert attempt. If you choose to restore, verify with git status and git log.

tool_input.command: git status --porcelain; git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD; echo '---LOG---'; git --no-pager log -n 20 --pretty=format:'%H %an %ai %s'

cd /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-refactored && 
BR=backup/revert-10f0a89f3 && 
# create branch from current HEAD (no checkout)
git branch "$BR" && 
echo "Created branch: $BR" && 
# show the new branch ref
git show-ref --heads "

cd /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-refactored && \
set -euo pipefail

COMMIT=10f0a89f3baa2bb9f2c0e6beae3cced1bd0489b5

# Move stray untracked file out of the way
if [ -f scripts/reset-shipyard-memory-target.sh ]; then
  echo "Mov

cd /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-refactored && \
REV=$(git rev-parse HEAD) && echo "Revert commit: $REV" && git --no-pager show --name-status --pretty=format:'%H %an %ai %s' $REV

cd /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-refactored && git restore --source=ce07cd3e859a9aab09a11b9cdd598557548faecd -- api/src/routes/feedback.ts pnpm-lock.yaml shared/package.json shared/src/index.ts shared/src/string-utils.test.ts s

cd /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-refactored && git restore --source=ce07cd3e859a9aab09a11b9cdd598557548faecd -- api/src/routes/feedback.ts pnpm-lock.yaml shared/package.json shared/src/index.ts shared/src/types/index.ts shared/

cd /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-refactored && git status --porcelain=2 --branch && echo '--- DIFFS vs parent ---' && for f in api/src/routes/feedback.ts pnpm-lock.yaml shared/package.json shared/src/index.ts shared/src/types/i

I read the contents of each listed file. Summary of what I observed in each:

- /.git/refs/heads/shipyard/1fb212d1-ad5e-45da-a674-cf12cf518c9c
  - Contains the commit hash (the ref for the shipyard run branch).

- api/src/routes/feedback.ts (modified)
  - Adds/updates publicFeedbackRouter endpoints for creating public feedback (POST /) and fetching program info for a public feedback form (GET /program/:programId).
  - Contains helper extractFeedbackFromRow.
  - Adds protected route to get a single feedback item (GET /:id) using authMiddleware.
  - Notes that accept/reject actions are handled elsewhere now.
  - Uses pool queries to read/insert documents and document_associations.

- pnpm-lock.yaml (modified)
  - Lockfile changes (large file). The commit modified the lockfile — exact package resolutions changed; I inspected the file head portion and index referencing @ship/shared workspace link.

- shared/package.json (modified)
  - Name: @ship/shared
  - Scripts: build, dev, clean, type-check, test, test:watch
  - Exports and types set (main: ./dist/index.js, types: ./dist/index.d.ts)
  - devDependencies include typescript, vitest, @types/node

- shared/src/index.ts (modified)
  - Re-exports from './types/index.js', './constants.js', './utils.js', './string-utils.js'

- shared/src/string-utils.test.ts (added)
  - Vitest tests for a capitalize function covering many cases (multiple spaces, special characters, apostrophes, numbers, uppercase input, etc.)

- shared/src/string-utils.ts (added)
  - Exports function capitalize(str: string): string
  - Uses regex: return str.replace(/\b(\w)(\w*)/g, transform) with transform that uppercases first char and lowercases rest.
  - Preserves empty string early return.

- shared/src/types/index.ts (modified)
  - Re-exports types including './user.js', './api.js', './db-rows.js', './auth.js', './workspace.js', './document.js', './fleetgraph.js', './tiptap.js'

- shared/src/types/user.ts (modified)
  - Exports interface User with fields: id, email, name, isSuperAdmin, lastWorkspaceId, createdAt (Date), updatedAt (Date)
  - Comments describe fields.

- shared/src/utils.test.ts (added)
  - Vitest tests for formatRelativeTime(date) with mocked system time; covers just now, minutes/hours/days ago, and future variants (in X minutes/hours/days).

- shared/src/utils.ts (added)
  - Exports function formatRelativeTime(date: Date): string
  - Computes diff to now and returns:
    - 'just now' for < 60 seconds
    - 'X minute(s) ago' or 'in X minutes' for minutes < 60
    - hours for < 24 hours
    - days for >= 24 hours

- shared/vitest.config.ts (added)
  - Defines vitest config: globals true, environment node, include tests under 'src/**/*.test.ts'

Notes / observations
- Several test and utility files were added under shared/src (string-utils and utils with corresponding tests and a vitest config).
- shared/src/index.ts now re-exports string-utils and utils (they are referenced with .js extension per repo convention).
- The API feedback router was touched to add public endpoints and helper logic.
- The lockfile (pnpm-lock.yaml) changed — likely to incorporate new/changed packages or workspace link resolutions.
- types changes: user type uses Date fields and camelCase field names (createdAt, updatedAt).
